rulings.Case Title: GARY E GROTE et al VS. DAVID DRIVER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Depositions Of Pltfs Gary Grote And Janice Grote Beyond The Distance Permitted Y Code Of Civil Procedure 2025.250
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 3, 2014, Line 1: DEFENDANTS DAVID DRIVER AND EMILIA RALLAPALLI'S Motion To Compel Depositions Of Plaintiffs Gary Grote And Janice Grote Beyond The Distance Permitted By Code Of Civil Procedure 2025.250 Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued to May 5, 2014 as a control date pending the outcome of the appeal of the December 20, 2011 judgment for Defendants Krishna Rallapalli and Philippine Rallapalli against Plaintiff Eren Hussein Upon Granting of Motion for Summary Judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: JAMES VINCENT DAWSON VS. OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 3, 2014, Line 7: DEFENDANTs OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY & JEFFREY RIZZO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment denied. Defendants met their initial burden of production that they did not breach a duty of care to Plaintiff. Plaintiff has raised a triable issue of material fact whether, despite the undisputed facts that Defendant Rizzo had the green light and the right of way at the time of the collision, he breached a duty of care owed to Plaintiff. See Leo v. Dunham (1953) 41 Cal. 2d 712, 715 ("general rule is that every person has a right to presume that every other person will perform his duty and obey the law, and in the absence of reasonable ground to think otherwise it is not negligence to assume that he is not exposed to danger which comes to him only from violation of law or duty by such other person")(emphasis added). See Declaration of Alvin Lowi in support of Plaintiff's opposition to motion, paras. 4-6); Declaration of Andrew Bryman in support of Plaintiff's opposition to motion, para. 2, Ex. 2 (Deposition of Defendant Jeffrey Rizzo), at 13:13-24; 15:17-23; 17:12-19:7; 19:14-24; 40:22-24; 43:14-44:8; 45:3-46:4; 46:23-47:18; 49:3-6; 51:25-52:3; 55:16-56:14; 57:18-20; 58:4-22; 66:4-11; 77:8-78:5; 79:22-80:9; 100:10-21;102:18-103:6; 103:15-23; 104:14-18; 23-25. The Court rules on Defendants' Evidentiary Objections to Plaintiff's Opposition to Motion for Summary Judgment as follows: objections to Declaration of Alvin Lowi: overruled; objections to Declaration of Andrew C. Bryman: sustained. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: CURTIS ANTHONY DAY VS. KARY WITT
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summ Adj
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 3, 2014, Line 12: DEFENDANT KARY WITT's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. The Court denies summary judgment and summary adjudication. Defendant's motion is not an improper reconsideration pursuant to CCP 437c(f)(2). Judge Nichols treated Defendant's prior motion for summary judgment as a motion for judgment on the pleadings and he did not entertain any of the parties' evidence. See Order filed August 14, 2013. The Court denies summary adjudication as to issues 1, 2, and 4. As to each issue, there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Mr. Witt's conduct was the result of personal animus against plaintiff and/or his union because of the negative postings regarding Mr. Witt that were on the union's website. See evidence cited in support of Plaintiff's Facts P8-P10 and P32-P33 (Declaration of Sarah Varela in Support of Opposition to Motion for Summary Judgment, 2 (Witt Deposition. pp. 121:23-122:14; 124:1-20; 127:24-128:1; 128:25-129:11; 129:17-25).) Defendant's objections to these portions of the Witt Deposition are overruled. (See Defendant's Objection to Plaintiff's Evidence, Objections 12-15.) The Court denies summary adjudication as to issue 3 because that issue (the propriety of injunctive relief) does not completely dispose of a cause of action as required by CCP 437c(f)(1). Plaintiff pleads one cause of action for violation Civil Code section 51.7 and remedies under related statutes. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: CRAIG GOLD VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 3, 2014, Line 14: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. Govt. Code sec. 818.4 grants immunity to governmental entities performing discretionary licensing and permitting functions. This immunity may be overcome by a violation of a mandatory duty pursuant to Govt. Code sec. 815.6. (Slagle Constr. Co. v. County of Contra Costa (1977) 67 Cal.App.3d 559, 562.) The statutes governing mobile food facility licensing and permitting use permissive language and are therefore discretionary. (San Francisco Municipal Police Code, Sec. 1320.8, "The Chief of Police may issue a permit¿" (emphasis added); San Francisco Department of Public Works Order No. 179,044, section I.5, "The Director may issue a MFF Permit¿" (emphasis added).) As such, the immunity of Govt. Code sec. 818.4 applies here, as the gravamen of the Third Amended Complaint is that Defendant wrongly revoked Plaintiff's food truck permit. The Third Amended Complaint pleads no other applicable mandatory duty. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend because Plaintiff has already been provided an opportunity to amend the complaint to name a mandatory duty and has failed to do so. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: MING HUA SHI VS. ROYAL TAXI, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 3, 2014, Line 3: PLAINTIFF MING SHI'S Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Within 15 days Defendant Sardar Arif Janbaz shall serve verified, code-compliant responses to Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories - Set One. Sanctions are granted in the amount of $1,230.00. The trial date in this matter is February 24, 2014. Plaintiff served the interrogatories on September 13, 2013 and, at Defendant's request, provided additional time to respond to the interrogatories. Defendant has nonetheless failed to provide responses to the interrogatories. A monetary sanction is mandatory against a party that unsuccessfully opposes a motion to compel a response to interrogatories, unless the Court "finds that the one subject to the sanctions acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." C.C.P. Section 2030.290(c). Here Defendant Janbaz has provided no evidence of circumstances that would render the imposition of sanctions unjust, or that Mr. Janbaz has acted with substantial justification in failing to provide verified responses. Defendant Janbaz shall pay Plaintiff $1,230.00 within 30 days of notice of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

(See Order Overruling Demurrer and Granting Motion to Stay filed June 26, 2013.) Therefore, the service of the summons and Cross-Complaint on Mr. Pak and the entry of default are void.
